audit_status.source_base: Greek Gospel text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for brothers, Booths feast, hiddenness, temple teaching, judgment, origin dispute, officers, living water, Spirit note, division, Nicodemus.

verses.restored: After these things Jesus was walking in Galilee, for he did not want to walk in Judea because the Judeans were seeking to kill him.
Now the Judean feast of Booths was near.
His brothers therefore said to him, Depart from here and go into Judea, so that your disciples also may behold your works that you do.
For no one does anything in hiddenness while seeking to be in openness. If you do these things, reveal yourself to the world.
For not even his brothers were trusting into him.
Jesus therefore says to them, My time is not yet present, but your time is always ready.
The world is not able to hate you; but it hates me, because I bear witness concerning it that its works are harmful.
You go up to the feast. I am not yet going up to this feast, because my time has not yet been fulfilled.
Having said these things to them, he remained in Galilee.
But when his brothers went up to the feast, then he also went up, not openly but as in hiddenness.
Therefore the Judeans were seeking him at the feast and saying, Where is that one?
There was much murmuring about him among the crowds. Some said, He is good; others said, No, but he leads the crowd astray.
Yet no one spoke openly about him because of fear of the Judeans.
When the feast was already halfway through, Jesus went up into the temple and began teaching.
The Judeans therefore marveled, saying, How does this one know writings, not having learned?
Jesus answered them and said, My teaching is not mine, but the One's who sent me.
If anyone wills to do that One's will, one will know concerning the teaching, whether it is from the Divine or whether I speak from myself.
The one speaking from oneself seeks one's own glory; but the one seeking the glory of the One who sent him, this one is true, and injustice is not in him.
Has not Moses given you the Law? Yet none of you does the Law. Why do you seek to kill me?
The crowd answered, You have a troubling spirit; who seeks to kill you?
Jesus answered and said to them, I did one work, and you all marvel.
Because of this Moses has given you circumcision, not that it is from Moses but from the fathers, and on a Sabbath you circumcise a human.
If a human receives circumcision on Sabbath so that the Law of Moses may not be broken, are you angry with me because I made a whole human healthy on Sabbath?
Do not judge according to appearance, but judge the righteous/just judgment.
Some of the Jerusalemites therefore said, Is this not the one they seek to kill?
Look, he speaks openly, and they say nothing to him. Have the rulers truly known that this is the Messiah?
But we know where this one is from; when the Messiah comes, no one knows where he is from.
Jesus therefore cried out in the temple, teaching and saying, You know me, and you know where I am from; yet I have not come from myself, but the One who sent me is true, whom you do not know.
I know that One, because I am from him, and that One sent me.
Therefore they were seeking to seize him, yet no one laid a hand on him, because his hour had not yet come.
But many from the crowd trusted into him and were saying, When the Messiah comes, will he do more signs than this one has done?
The Pharisees heard the crowd murmuring these things about him, and the chief priests and Pharisees sent officers to seize him.
Jesus therefore said, Yet a little time I am with you, and I go to the One who sent me.
You will seek me and will not find me; and where I am, you are not able to come.
The Judeans therefore said to one another, Where is this one about to go, that we will not find him? Is he about to go to the dispersion among the Greeks and teach the Greeks?
What is this word that he said, You will seek me and will not find me, and where I am, you are not able to come?
On the last day, the great day of the feast, Jesus stood and cried out, saying, If anyone thirsts, let that one come to me and drink.
The one trusting into me, just as the Scripture said, rivers of living water will flow from within that one.
He said this concerning the Spirit, whom those trusting into him were about to receive; for Spirit was not yet given, because Jesus had not yet been glorified.
Some from the crowd, hearing these words, were saying, This is truly the Prophet.
Others were saying, This is the Messiah. But others said, Does the Messiah come from Galilee?
Has not the Scripture said that the Messiah comes from the seed of David and from Bethlehem, the village where David was?
Therefore division happened in the crowd because of him.
Some of them wanted to seize him, but no one laid hands on him.
The officers therefore came to the chief priests and Pharisees, and those said to them, Why did you not bring him?
The officers answered, Never has a human spoken like this.
The Pharisees therefore answered them, Have you also been led astray?
Has anyone from the rulers or from the Pharisees trusted into him?
But this crowd that does not know the Law is cursed.
Nicodemus, the one who had come to him earlier, being one of them, says to them,
Does our Law judge a human unless it first hears from that one and knows what that one is doing?
They answered and said to him, Are you also from Galilee? Search and see that no prophet arises from Galilee.
[[Then each went to his own house. This verse begins the later textual tradition continued in 8:1-11.]]
